title: Fix Daily Page date selection to use Indian Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30)

requirements.text: Fix the 'Mark all travellers for today (AM & PM)' checkbox on the Daily Page (DailyParticipationGrid.tsx) so that 'today' is determined using Indian Standard Time (IST, UTC+5:30) instead of UTC. Any call to get the current date (e.g., `new Date()`) used for this checkbox's logic must apply a +5:30 offset to produce the correct IST date string (YYYY-MM-DD key format).

quotes: On Daily Page when i am selecting check box it select a day ahead instead current date
make last requested changes related to time

acceptanceCriteria: When the 'Mark all travellers for today' checkbox is clicked, it marks trips for the current calendar date in IST (UTC+5:30), not UTC.
At any time of day in India, the checkbox selects the correct local date and does not jump to the next day.
No other Daily Page functionality is broken by this change.
The IST date derivation is consistent with any shared date utility (dateRange.ts) — refactor to a shared IST 'today' helper if one does not already exist.

requirements.text: Audit all other usages of `new Date()` (or equivalent) across the frontend where today's date is derived and displayed or used as a default value (e.g., default date in payment forms, expense forms, OtherPendingAmountForm, CashPaymentForm, DateRangePicker defaults, auto-toll date logic). Ensure every such usage also applies the IST (UTC+5:30) offset so the displayed or pre-filled date is always the correct current date in India.

quotes: Why today's date is taking 1 day ahead
make last requested changes related to time

acceptanceCriteria: All date inputs that default to 'today' across the app show the correct IST date.
A single reusable IST 'today' utility function (e.g., `getTodayIST()`) is used consistently so timezone logic is not duplicated.
No form or page shows a date that is one day ahead of the actual IST date.

constraints: Do not modify any files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
Do not change backend data models or stored date formats — only fix how the frontend derives the current date for display and defaults.
IST offset must be hardcoded as UTC+5:30 (19800 seconds); do not rely on the user's device timezone setting.
nonGoals: Changing how historical dates (already saved trip entries) are stored or displayed.
Adding timezone selection UI for users.
